heading: Failure to comply with agreements
label: 290cc–31 Failure to comply with agreements
notes: Amendments1990— amended section generally, substituting provisions relating to failure to comply with agreements for provision relating to technical assistance.Pub. L. 101–645
amendments: Amendments1990— amended section generally, substituting provisions relating to failure to comply with agreements for provision relating to technical assistance.Pub. L. 101–645

children.text: The Secretary may, subject to subsection (c), require a State to repay any payments received by the State under that the Secretary determines were not expended by the State in accordance with the agreements required to be contained in the application submitted by the State pursuant to .section 290cc–21 of this titlesection 290cc–29 of this title

If a State fails to make a repayment required in paragraph (1), the Secretary may offset the amount of the repayment against the amount of any payment due to be paid to the State under .section 290cc–21 of this title

The Secretary may, subject to subsection (c), withhold payments due under if the Secretary determines that the State involved is not expending amounts received under such section in accordance with the agreements required to be contained in the application submitted by the State pursuant to .section 290cc–21 of this titlesection 290cc–29 of this title

The Secretary shall cease withholding payments from a State under paragraph (1) if the Secretary determines that there are reasonable assurances that the State will expend amounts received under in accordance with the agreements referred to in such paragraph.section 290cc–21 of this title
The Secretary may not withhold funds under paragraph (1) from a State for a minor failure to comply with the agreements referred to in such paragraph.

children.heading: Opportunity for hearing
children.label: c Opportunity for hearing
children.text: Before requiring repayment of payments under subsection (a)(1), or withholding payments under subsection (b)(1), the Secretary shall provide to the State an opportunity for a hearing.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: d Rule of construction
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this part, a State receiving payments under may not, with respect to any agreements required to be contained in the application submitted under , be considered to be in violation of any such agreements by reason of the fact that the State, in the regular course of providing services under to eligible homeless individuals, incidentally provides services to homeless individuals who are not eligible homeless individuals.section 290cc–21 of this titlesection 290cc–29 of this titlesection 290cc–22(b) of this title
